per_file.covers: Suite-wide fixtures: (1) autouse _simulate_linux_for_unit_tests — forces cli.IS_MACOS=False/IS_LINUX=True on every non-slow test across 6 cli submodules (patches each submodule's copied constant), clears YOLO_RUNTIME/YOLO_VERSION; (2) autouse _hermetic_storage_paths — rebinds 8 storage constants (GLOBAL_STORAGE/HOME/MISE/CACHE, CONTAINER_DIR, AGENTS_DIR, BUILD_DIR, USER_CONFIG_PATH) + 3 broker singleton /tmp paths to a per-test tmp root, across BOTH import aliases ('cli.*' and 'src.cli.*') and _host_mise_dir; (3) sock_dir — short /tmp dir for AF_UNIX (sun_path 108/104-byte cap); (4) session-scoped ensure_jail_image — when running INSIDE a jail with slow tests selected, nix-builds .#ociImage via host nix daemon and pipes the streamLayeredImage script into `podman load` (skips entirely for -m 'not slow'); sets MISE_DATA_DIR at import time (entrypoint requires it).

5400 lines, 60 classes. Pure/mockable cli.py logic: argv routing, bare-yolo option forwarding, repo-root resolution, container naming/tracking/find/remove-stale, startup banner, version, port-forward parsing, MCP server names, mise-tool merge, blocked-tool normalization, nix progress formatting, sentinel files, JSONC loading, load_config/merge_config/_validate_config, effective packages, dotenv parsing, env_sources, workspace-readonly mounts, preset-null conflicts, runtime-for-check, nix custom conf, nix daemon label detect, host timezone detect, ensure_global_storage, project name, generate AGENTS.md, prepare skills, config changes, seed agent dir, entrypoint preflight, CliRunner smoke, image size estimate, cgroup validation/daemon ops/socket, journal daemon, host services, broker singleton, broker relay supervision (mocked spawn), relay orphan sweep, host-nix mount decision, GPU/ROCm host detection, host-service liveness probe, broker creds freshness, scratch/ro-file mount args, run-with-proxy, proxy teardown signal, orphaned-jail reaper, pid-alive, stop-jail, LSP install resolution.

4100 lines, 47 classes. Heavier cli.py flows via typer CliRunner with @patch('subprocess.run'/'subprocess.Popen'/'subprocess.check_output'/'shutil.which'): auto_load_image build+stream+load pipeline and sentinel logic; image store path; tmux/kitty pane setup; `yolo check --no-build` sections (global storage warnings, user-config error, flake not found, runtime error, repo-path-in-workspace, entrypoint preflight, nix build section, image+containers, failure summary, broker-relay probe); doctor alias; ps; host port forwarding (socat); init (user config, briefing); run internals: config errors, identity env collection, YOLO flag injection, exec-into-existing, broker-relay wiring, new-container mounts/network (asserts flags like '--net=host', '-p 8000:8000' inside mock_popen.call_args[0][0]), repo mount source, podman specifics, device/KVM/ROCm passthrough, profile, AGENTS.md edge cases, refresh-jail-briefings, seed-agent-dir, sync-claude-json, port-forward cleanup/errors, main().

3300 lines, 31 classes. Entrypoint config generation with module path constants rebound to tmp_path (jail_home fixture; clears YOLO_* env leakage): shim generation (blocked tools), .bashrc, CA bundle, ld cache, timezone, per-agent configs (copilot/gemini/claude/opencode/pi/codex incl. MCP files, Claude host-settings snapshot, shared credentials dir), MCP wrappers, mise config, bootstrap script, agent/package-manager launchers, in-container port forwarding, port-in-use, perf logging, venv precreate script, git/jj config, LSP server loading, mise config update, exec-bash (os.execvp patched), cglimit script, cgroup delegation, main() flow, workspace-config trust, supervisor single-instance, user env hydration. Assertions are file-content/structure checks on generated files.

load_config/merge_config/_validate_config/_load_jsonc_with_includes/_check_config_changes with real JSONC files in tmp_path: list dedup + scalar override, agents override-not-union + claude-only default, mcpServers dict merge + null-disable, strict JSONC errors, unknown top-level key rejection, lsp extension requirement, env_sources validation + user-then-workspace concat, ephemeral_storage validation, same-file preset/null conflict vs cross-hierarchy allowed, seed_agent_dir auth-file copy, include-if-found, workspace-local config (.local overlay), MCP requires_env, agents_md_extra, per-side paths, .yolo/config-snapshot.json lifecycle, loophole overrides.

THE e2e suite (pytestmark = slow, ~36 tests). Spawns real containers via `uv run --project REPO python -m src.cli run -- bash -lc <cmd>` from a temp project with yolo-jail.jsonc (agents copilot/gemini/claude, blocked curl/grep, bridge net); 300s default timeout (YOLO_TEST_JAIL_TIMEOUT); teardown force-removes container using a REIMPLEMENTED copy of container_name_for_workspace (sha256 hash naming). Covers: blocked-tool messages/exit, yolo init/check (valid + invalid fails before start), shim persistence, agent CLIs on PATH (login + non-login shell), agent version pins + config propagation, agent-selection pruning, direct `yolo -- cmd`, in-jail configs present, in-jail `yolo check`, extra-package libs in lib farm/ldcache/dlopen, dev-package runtime lib links, in-jail `yolo --help`, MCP server/preset propagation + same-file conflict rejection, per-workspace MCP isolation, AGENTS.md placement, host-venv shadowing, mise store writability + venv activation, vscode MCP shadowing, overmind socket isolation, host port-forwarding data, cgroup delegation + cglimit CPU enforcement (skipped if cgroupfs read-only, i.e. nested).

Runtime selection: YOLO_RUNTIME env > config > auto-detect; invalid value rejection; check/config-ref/AGENTS.md must all mention 'yolo check after every config edit'; list_jails parsing for apple `container ls` table vs `podman ps --filter`; per-runtime image-load sentinel; skip load when container running; exec path; native macos-user runtime gating; --dry-run rejected for container runtimes. PLUS 7 @slow integration tests parametrized over AVAILABLE_RUNTIMES (podman detected via `podman info`, apple container via `container system status`): basic command, blocked tool, file ownership, workspace mount, stale-container auto-remove/--new, startup banner.

Host<->jail state separation: /mise store mount + env contract, nested-jail store rule, per-side venv shadow mounts, layout-v2 storage migration, jail-made venv retirement, _live_yolo_containers, zero-live-jails store-prune gate, provisioning shell (validated with real `bash -n` syntax check on the generated script).

src/prune.py: workspace discovery via container-runtime metadata (MagicMock runtime calls), dedupable-file walking, cache purge by age, image-cache prune, shadowed-home prune, global dedup walk, hardlink dedup, stopped-container prune, old-image prune, disk-usage report, `yolo prune` CLI command (dry-run default), doctor disk-usage check, orphan build roots. Fake directory trees built in tmp_path; no real containers.

src/loopholes.py registry: manifest discovery/parsing from real manifest.jsonc files in tmp dirs — name-dir match, disabled skip, transport/lifecycle/restart validation, runtime_args (TLS intercept + CA env, skip on apple container, config-backed skip), CA path merge, set_enabled roundtrip, jail_daemon/host_daemon specs, requires predicates (command_on_path, file_exists with env expansion), in-jail vs host activation branches (YOLO_VERSION), host_bind_mounts (env + {loophole_dir} template expansion, missing-source skip, emitted -v args), host_devices (--device flags, in-jail skip, missing-node skip), bundled audio loophole parsing + /dev/snd emission, workspace overrides merge, bundled-vs-user precedence, doctor checks.

macOS branches of cli.py run on Linux CI by setting IS_MACOS=True/IS_LINUX=False + mocking runtime/nix/subprocess: cgroup skip, mise named-volume instead of bind, port forwarding, device/GPU/KVM skip, container runtime selection, tmpfs flags, macOS doctor, Apple Container runtime specifics + mount workarounds, macOS broker relay.

macos-user (Seatbelt) backend pure artifact generators, asserted on Linux: seatbelt profile text (write/read denials, keychain, raw disk, packet capture), launch argv (env -i scrub, HOME/USER/SHELL not overridable), user provisioning + hidden account, workspace ACL dir/file split, entrypoint bootstrap, orchestrator guards, setup, log helper, broker socket grant, sandbox env, python resolution, no-sudo policy (refuse under sudo — latest commit), stage entrypoint, workspace location, bootstrap hardening/argv, run plan, darwin packages in plan, --dry-run output.

src/cli/darwin_packages.py: nix build argv for darwin buildenv + skip-list eval, system override, packages-JSON env marshaling, profile bin/pkgconfig paths, locked nixpkgs rev from flake.lock (also cross-checked against the repo's real lock), materialize() with monkeypatched subprocess.run (success, nix missing, build failure, empty out, nonfatal skip-list failure).

macOS Linux-builder (src/cli/builder.py): ssh config block, nix builders line, trusted-users merge line, batched root setup script + single-sudo pipe, reachability via mocked socket, setup-state probing, ensure orchestration (start + poll to ready, first-boot key flow incl. Ctrl-C, timeout with log tail), status.

src/host_service.py framed-JSON-over-AF_UNIX daemon library, tested against REAL sockets with in-thread servers: request/response roundtrip (stdout + exit frames), session JSON line, handler exception -> stderr + exit 1, exec_allowlisted enforcement, socket mode 0600, concurrent connections, default exit 0. Protocol framing: 4-byte struct length prefix + JSON body.

host-processes loophole daemon over real sockets: list mode runs real `ps` for allowlisted comm (Linux-only — BSD ps lacks -C), pid mode rejects non-allowlisted comm / bad pid, empty visible list, unknown mode, self_check variants, yolo-ps socket env convention.

Host OAuth broker: cached-token freshness/expiry/corruption, do_refresh cache hit/miss with mocked urllib upstream, never touches host creds file, no_refresh_token error dict, do_proxy (b64 body, hop-by-hop header strip, gzip warning, HTTPError passthrough, network-failure error dict, path validation, User-Agent rules), authorization_code propagation to shared creds (and all the do-NOT-write cases: non-token path, non-200, transport error, non-token-shaped/unparseable body — via read_text snapshots), ping handler, decode_proxy_request shape validation, normalize_oauth scope/subscription/refresh-token merge semantics, write_tokens atomic rename, ensure_ca via REAL openssl (generate/idempotent/force-rotate/missing-openssl error).

In-jail TLS terminator: _is_refresh_grant classification (only grant_type=refresh_token goes to host refresh; authorization_code and non-token paths proxy upstream THROUGH the broker socket, never direct — --add-host loops platform.claude.com back), proxy_upstream 502 shapes (host broker failure, upstream error dict, missing socket), _ask_host_broker error-layer naming (relay ENOENT/refused vs broker EOF/reset) using real sockets in sock_dir.

broker_relay.py run AS A REAL SUBPROCESS (launched by file path, stdlib-only) against an in-test thread FakeBroker speaking the framed protocol: broker restart re-bind (new inode) reachable on next connect (Round-2 regression), jail_id stamping overrides client value, unparseable/non-JSON frames forwarded verbatim, broker-down EOF + layer naming, no fd growth across sequential connections, _relay_ensure idempotence + heal after kill -9 + _relay_stop reaping.
Generated agent-briefing (AGENTS.md) content: provisioning-failure section, conditional sections (loopholes/resources/dev-workspace appear only when data exists), briefing slimness guarantees.

yolo-doctor formatting helpers: splitter turning broker self-check blob into (title, detail) pairs (empty, single, multiple, preamble ignored, blank continuation stripped), finalize, cross-build diagnosis pointing at Linux builder. Structure only — 'rendering is visual', byte output deliberately not asserted.

Podman Machine memory advisories: _podman_machine_memory parsing of `podman machine inspect` JSON, resize hint, `yolo check` warning below floor / below resources.memory, `yolo run` OOM hint on exit 137 (macOS+podman).

Regressions: _check_loopholes skips doctor_cmd for loopholes whose requires predicate is unmet; broker self_check returns 0 (warning) when CA/leaf state missing pre-deploy, fails only when openssl AND state both missing.

Regressions from an external handoff: host_processes.visible accepted as top-level config key, its type validated, unknown subkey rejected; check skips loophole exec-check inside jail; no 'host_services' wording in user-facing text.
In-jail daemon supervisor: _parse_env robustness (empty/non-JSON/wrong type), plus small real-subprocess bits.
Fast-suite guardrails on test_jail.py's own constants: DEFAULT_JAIL_TIMEOUT floor (300s; locks the 240s empirical CI cold-start floor so a revert can't re-redden CI).

style: Pytest + typer.testing.CliRunner + unittest.mock. Three tiers: (1) mock-heavy CLI unit tests — podman/nix/socat are faked by @patch('subprocess.run'/'subprocess.Popen'/'subprocess.check_output'/'shutil.which') at module scope; the generated podman argv is recovered from mock_popen.call_args[0][0] and asserted flag-by-flag (substring membership, never full-argv equality); (2) real-fs generation tests (entrypoint, config merge, loopholes, prune, macos_user) that rebind module-level path constants to tmp_path and assert generated file content/structure; (3) real-socket/subprocess protocol tests (host_service, host_processes, oauth_broker_jail, broker_relay) using in-thread AF_UNIX servers and the relay as an actual child process. Two autouse conftest fixtures make everything hermetic and Linux-simulated: they monkeypatch IS_MACOS/IS_LINUX plus 11 storage/broker path constants ACROSS EVERY MODULE ALIAS ('cli.*' AND 'src.cli.*' — the suite imports the same source under two module identities). PORT WARNING: this whole hermeticity mechanism is Python-only (runtime rebinding of module globals, dual import aliases, monkeypatching constants copied into submodule namespaces at import time). A Go binary cannot be tested this way — it needs explicit override hooks (env vars like YOLO_STORAGE_ROOT, or a fake `podman` shim script on PATH that records argv) to reproduce the same hermetic surface. The mock-at-call_args argv assertions translate naturally to a PATH-shim that dumps argv to a file. macOS tests run on Linux entirely via mocks; Darwin-privileged code paths (Seatbelt apply, launchctl, /etc/nix writes) are asserted only as the COMMAND STRINGS they would construct, verified manually on real Macs via docs/runbooks/. pytest marker: 'slow' = spins real containers; fast suite runs with -n 4 --dist worksteal (xdist), slow suite strictly serial (session-scoped image load must not run per worker).
runner: Justfile: `just test` = `uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/` (serial, includes slow); `just test-fast` = `pytest tests/ -m "not slow" -n 4 --dist worksteal`; `just check` = format+lint+test-fast; `just check-ci` = lint-ci+test-fast (pre-commit); `just check-all` = full incl. containers. CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml): job `check` (ubuntu) + `check-macos` run `uv run pytest tests/ -v -m "not slow" -n 4 --dist worksteal`; job `build-image` (matrix ubuntu-latest + ubuntu-24.04-arm) nix-builds .#ociImageMinimal and uploads the tar; job `integration` (same matrix) installs podman+socat, `podman load` the artifact, then `uv run pytest tests/test_jail.py -v` with YOLO_RUNTIME=podman. nightly-macos.yml: macos-26-intel runner (nested virt for Podman Machine) runs `uv run pytest tests/test_jail.py tests/test_runtime.py -v` against the Linux-built full image. pytest config in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options]: only the 'slow' marker. Dev deps: pytest>=9, pytest-xdist, pytest-cov, coverage, ruff (coverage not gated in CI).
e2e_story: Real-container e2e exists and is substantial: tests/test_jail.py (~36 tests, all `slow`) launches actual jails via `uv run --project REPO python -m src.cli run -- bash -lc <cmd>` from temp projects, 300s timeout, covering blocked-tool shims, agent installs (copilot/gemini/claude versions + config propagation), MCP propagation/isolation, lib-farm/ldcache for extra packages, venv shadowing, mise store, cgroup delegation + cglimit CPU enforcement, in-jail `yolo check`/`--help`. test_runtime.py adds 7 slow tests parametrized over runtimes actually present (podman; apple `container` when its system is running — so the Apple runtime is only e2e-tested on a dev Mac, never in CI). CI runs test_jail.py on real rootless podman on BOTH x86_64 and arm64 nightly-ish (every push, after image build), and nightly on macOS Intel via Podman Machine. Nested-jail support is first-class: conftest's session fixture detects in-container execution, builds the image through the host nix daemon socket, and streams it into the inner podman; cgroup tests self-skip when cgroupfs is read-only (nested). CLAUDE.md additionally mandates manual nested-jail verification (`yolo -- bash` from inside a jail) for src/cli//src/entrypoint changes — an undocumented-in-tests human e2e layer. macOS Darwin-privileged paths (Seatbelt, launchctl, Apple container mounts) have NO automated e2e; they rely on docs/runbooks/ manual Mac testing.
gaps: Zero golden/snapshot tests anywhere — every assertion is hand-written substring/structure membership; no fixture-config corpus, no byte-exact expected outputs to diff a Go port against.

Generated container argv is never asserted as a complete ordered list — only individual flags ('--net=host' in cmd). Flag order, the full default mount set, and unasserted flags are unpinned and could silently change in a port.

Rich-rendered output (yolo check/doctor tables, banners) explicitly untested byte-wise ('rendering is visual so we test the structure') — the highest-visibility user surface has the weakest parity net.

container_name_for_workspace is REIMPLEMENTED in test_jail.py rather than imported — the naming algorithm (lowercase, [^a-z0-9-]->'-', strip, [:40], sha256(path)[:8]) is a cross-language contract with no single source of truth.
Apple `container` runtime: unit-mocked only on CI; real e2e requires a dev Mac with the runtime running (AVAILABLE_RUNTIMES probe). No CI coverage.
macos-user backend privileged apply (dscl/ACL/sandbox-exec/launchctl) untested by automation — only the constructed command strings are asserted; behavior verified via manual runbooks.
entrypoint main() as PID-1 init is only unit-tested with os.execvp patched; the real boot sequence is covered solely by the slow test_jail.py path.

conftest hermeticity depends on Python module-global monkeypatching across dual import aliases — extremely fragile (comments document past leaks: 1650 litter dirs in real AGENTS_DIR, live-broker /tmp collisions) and has NO equivalent for a Go binary; port needs env-var path-override hooks designed in from day one.
Framed unix-socket protocol (4-byte length prefix + JSON) is tested per-daemon but there is no single wire-format contract test/fixture set to validate a Go implementation against.
No coverage gate in CI; pytest-cov installed but unused in workflows.
Fast suite depends on xdist worksteal with exactly -n 4; tests that accidentally share machine-global state would only surface as flakes.
yolo config-ref / --help full text only substring-checked (test_runtime.py checks two phrases).
characterization_candidates: `yolo check --no-build` full stdout+stderr+exit code over a fixture-config corpus (valid; unknown top-level key; unknown agent; bad runtime; legacy env key; bad lsp entry; invalid ephemeral_storage; missing flake; host_processes.visible present) — run with TERM=dumb/NO_COLOR to make rich output deterministic; currently only substring-asserted in TestCheckCommand*/test_config_merge.

Config-merge result as canonical JSON: for each (user config, workspace config, workspace .local, includes, presets, null-overrides) fixture pair, dump merge_config/load_config output — locks list-dedup, agents override-not-union, mcpServers merge + null-disable, env_sources user-then-workspace concat, preset/null same-file conflict ERROR TEXT.
_validate_config error message text and exit code for every rejection case — these strings are user-visible contract (tests already assert fragments; golden the whole message).

FULL generated `podman run` argv (ordered) for canonical configs: default, bridge+ports, host net, extra mounts, readonly workspace, scratch/ro-file mounts, devices/KVM/GPU/ROCm, loophole runtime_args (TLS intercept CA env, host_bind_mounts with {loophole_dir}, --device flags), venv shadow mounts, /mise store mount, macOS variants (mise named volume, tmpfs, Apple-container mount workarounds, cgroup skip). Capture via a fake `podman` PATH shim that dumps argv — the single highest-value parity artifact; today only flag-membership is asserted.

Exact set of YOLO_* env vars injected into the container (-e flags / env file) per fixture config — the CLI->entrypoint contract that a Go CLI must reproduce for the Python entrypoint to keep working (this is THE seam for incremental porting).

Entrypoint-generated file tree: given a fixed YOLO_* env input set, hash/golden every generated file under the fake HOME — shims (incl. blocked-tool message/suggestion text and exit code), .bashrc, copilot/gemini/claude/opencode/pi/codex config JSONs, yolo-managed-mcp-servers.json, mise config.toml, MCP wrapper scripts, CA bundle, bootstrap + venv-precreate + cglimit scripts (also `bash -n` them, as test_state_separation already does for the provisioning shell).

Generated AGENTS.md / agent briefing for fixture states (loopholes enabled, resources set, provisioning-failure marker, yolo-jail dev workspace, agents_md_extra) — full-text golden; must include the 'yolo check after every config edit' sentence (test_runtime.py guards it).

container_name_for_workspace(path) for a table of paths (unicode, long names, punctuation) — byte-identical naming is required or Go loses track of Python-created containers; test_jail.py's duplicate implementation proves it's a contract.

Framed-socket wire fixtures: recorded request/response byte streams for host_service (length-prefixed JSON frames: stdout/session/exit/stderr), broker relay jail_id stamping, oauth broker actions (ping->pong; refresh with cache hit/miss; proxy request/response b64 shapes; error dicts no_refresh_token / upstream_unreachable / 502 shapes; hop-by-hop header stripping; User-Agent rules) — replay against both implementations.

normalize_oauth output table (scope string vs scopes list, subscription preservation, previous-refresh-token retention, whitespace splitting) and the credentials-file JSON format written by write_tokens (atomic rename).
loopholes runtime_args output (mount/device/env argv fragments) per manifest fixture, incl. env/{loophole_dir} expansion and in-jail vs host branches.
prune dry-run report text and disk-usage report for a canonical fake tree (sizes pinned).

Pure-parser outputs as tables: _parse_port_forwards, _parse_dotenv, merge_mise_tools, normalize_blocked_tools, _summarize_nix_line/format_progress, list_jails parsing of podman-ps and apple `container ls` fixture outputs, _podman_machine_memory inspect-JSON parsing.
.yolo/config-snapshot.json content and change-detection behavior (_check_config_changes true/false matrix).

macos_user artifacts: full seatbelt profile text, launch argv (env -i ordering), setup/bootstrap scripts, ACL command list; builder.py ssh-config block, nix builders line, trusted-users line, batched root setup script — all pure string generators, ideal for byte-exact goldens.
Startup banner text and doctor problem-splitter (title, detail) pairs for fixture broker blobs.
